title: Ventilago ecorollata
taxonomy: Ventilago ecorollata is a species in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as the buckthorn or sumac family. This family consists of approximately 950 species of flowering plants, with the majority being deciduous shrubs or small trees. Ventilago ecorollata is native to Australia, where it grows as a dense shrub or small tree, often found in coastal forests and heathlands. The classification of Ventilago ecorollata places it within the genus Ventilago, which consists of approximately 30 species. Further classification includes its order, family, and kingdom, with Ventilago ecorollata being a member of the order Rosales, family Rhamnaceae, and kingdom Plantae.
uses: Ventilago ecorollata, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties.
